claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 16 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have two children - Alex, who's 18, and Blake, who's 13. I'm looking to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have two children - Alex (18) and Blake (13).

Let me start by asking about Alex, who is 18. Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. They finished school and aren't doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying about Alex. 

Now let me ask about Blake, who is 13. Does Blake live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Blake does spend time at home with me - they come home regularly and stay with me. But their other parent also lives with them, and they're the one who already claims Child Benefit for Blake.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's an important detail.

Just to clarify - when you say Blake's other parent lives with them and claims Child Benefit, does that mean Blake primarily lives with their other parent, and just visits you regularly?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. Blake primarily lives with their other parent, and I see them regularly when they visit me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Let me ask one more question to complete the assessment:

Do you make any financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep (such as for clothes, food, pocket money, or helping with accommodation costs) that equal or exceed the Child Benefit amount (which is £25.60 per week for the first child or £16.95 per week for additional children)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't currently contribute financially towards Blake's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for either of your children. Here's why:

**For Alex (18 years old):**
You are not eligible because Alex is over 16 and is not currently in approved education or training. Child Benefit normally covers children under 16, or under 20 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex has finished school and isn't doing any courses, the eligibility criteria are not met.

**For Blake (13 years old):**
You are not eligible because Blake's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child. Additionally, Blake primarily lives with their other parent (you only have visits), and you are not making financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep that equal or exceed the Child Benefit amount.

**Please note:** If Alex were to start approved education or training in the future, you could potentially claim Child Benefit for them at that point. Similarly, if circumstances changed with Blake (for example, if they came to live with you and the other parent stopped claiming), you could potentially become eligible then.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or your situation?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is not in approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for children under 16, or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and not in approved education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 13 years old and primarily lives with their other parent, who is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Under Child Benefit rules, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child. Since Blake's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and you do not live with Blake (they only visit you), and you are not making financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep that equal or exceed the Child Benefit amount, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children: Alex (18) and Blake (13). For Alex, eligibility cannot be established because Alex is over 16 and not in approved education or training. For Blake, although they are under 16, the other parent already claims Child Benefit and Blake primarily lives with that parent. The claimant only has visits from Blake and does not contribute financially to their upkeep. Since only one person can claim Child Benefit per child, and the claimant does not meet the criteria for continued eligibility (living with the child or making sufficient financial contributions), they are not eligible for Child Benefit for either child.

Since Blake's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and you do not live with Blake (they only visit you), and you are not making financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep that equal or exceed the Child Benefit amount, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

For Blake, although they are under 16, the other parent already claims Child Benefit and Blake primarily lives with that parent. The claimant only has visits from Blake and does not contribute financially to their upkeep. Since only one person can claim Child Benefit per child, and the claimant does not meet the criteria for continued eligibility (living with the child or making sufficient financial contributions), they are not eligible for Child Benefit for either child.
